title: festuca inermis subsp. villosa
intro: Festuca inermis subsp. villosa, also known as the bearded fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The bearded fescue has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Festuca inermis subsp. villosa include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
